title: ptelea trifoliata subsp. trifoliata
intro: Ptelea trifoliata subsp. trifoliata, also known as the Hog Peanut or Eastern Prickly-ash, is a plant species native to eastern North America, characterized by its prickly stems and three-lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Ptelea trifoliata subsp. trifoliata include its potential to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
